name: breadcrumb
title: Breadcrumb
description: Shows where the current page sits in the site hierarchy as a trail of links inside a nav landmark. The current page is a span with aria-current="page", never a link. Links render the Commons Link (default variant), keeping the underline, visited-state, and forced-colors contract.
useWhen: Pages two or more levels deep in a hierarchy (service → category → detail)
Helping users jump back up to any ancestor level in one activation
Orienting users who arrive deep in the site from search results or shared links
avoidWhen: Single-level sites or landing pages — a trail of one item orients no one
Replacing primary navigation or a step indicator in a multi-step form (steps are a sequence, not a hierarchy)
Trails that need collapsing/truncation of middle items — not in v1; long trails wrap onto new lines instead (flex-wrap)

keyboard: Tab moves through the breadcrumb links in document order; Shift+Tab moves backwards
Enter activates a link and follows the href (native <a> contract)
The current page is a <span aria-current="page">, not a link — it is skipped by Tab and never focusable
Separators are aria-hidden decoration inside each list item — never announced, never focusable
accessibility.targetSize: Breadcrumb links are inline text and use the WCAG 2.5.8 inline exception; the trail is an orientation aid, not an action bar
